Learn React

Real-World Use Cases
Home Use Cases Games Projects

15 Production Use Cases

Patterns you'll build in every real React application. Each card shows the core hook/component architecture with key implementation details.

01 / DATA

Data Table with Sorting & Filtering

Admin dashboards, CMS, analytics
function useTableState<T>(data: T[]) {
  const [sort, setSort] = useState<{
    key: keyof T; dir: "asc" | "desc"
  } | null>(null);
  const [filters, setFilters] = useState<
    Partial<Record<keyof T, string>>
  >({});

  const processed = useMemo(() => {
    let result = data.filter(row =>
      Object.entries(filters).every(([k, v]) =>
        String(row[k]).includes(v as string)
      )
    );
    if (sort) result.sort(/* ... */);
    return result;
  }, [data, sort, filters]);

  return { processed, sort, setSort, filters, setFilters };
}
  • useMemo prevents re-sorting/filtering on unrelated renders
  • Type-safe column definitions with keyof T for sort keys
  • Composable: add pagination, selection, and column resize as separate hooks
useMemo Generics Custom Hook
02 / UX

Infinite Scroll Feed

Social feeds, news, product listings
function useInfiniteScroll(fetchFn, opts) {
  const [pages, setPages] = useState([]);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);
  const observer = useRef<IntersectionObserver>();

  const sentinelRef = useCallback(node => {
    if (loading) return;
    if (observer.current) observer.current.disconnect();
    observer.current = new IntersectionObserver(entries => {
      if (entries[0].isIntersecting) loadMore();
    }, { rootMargin: "200px" });
    if (node) observer.current.observe(node);
  }, [loading]);

  return { pages, loading, sentinelRef, hasMore };
}

// Usage:
const { pages, sentinelRef } = useInfiniteScroll(fetchPosts);
// Place <div ref={sentinelRef} /> at list bottom
  • IntersectionObserver triggers load 200px before sentinel is visible
  • useCallback ref pattern handles dynamic sentinel mounting
  • Pages array enables "load more" and scroll restoration
IntersectionObserver useCallback ref Pagination
03 / FORMS

Multi-step Form Wizard

Onboarding, checkout, registration
type Step = { id: string; validate: () => boolean };

function useFormWizard(steps: Step[]) {
  const [currentStep, setStep] = useState(0);
  const [formData, setFormData] = useState<Record<string, any>>({});
  const [errors, setErrors] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});

  const next = () => {
    if (steps[currentStep].validate()) {
      setStep(s => Math.min(s + 1, steps.length - 1));
    }
  };

  const prev = () => setStep(s => Math.max(s - 1, 0));
  const progress = (currentStep + 1) / steps.length;

  return { currentStep, formData, setFormData,
    errors, next, prev, progress };
}
  • Per-step validation prevents advancing with invalid data
  • Centralized formData object shared across all steps
  • Progress indicator computed from current/total steps
useReducer Validation Step Navigation
04 / REAL-TIME

Real-time Dashboard

Monitoring, analytics, trading platforms
function useWebSocket<T>(url: string) {
  const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<
    "connecting" | "open" | "closed"
  >("connecting");
  const wsRef = useRef<WebSocket>();

  useEffect(() => {
    const ws = new WebSocket(url);
    wsRef.current = ws;
    ws.onopen = () => setStatus("open");
    ws.onmessage = (e) => setData(JSON.parse(e.data));
    ws.onclose = () => {
      setStatus("closed");
      // Auto-reconnect with backoff
      setTimeout(() => reconnect(), delay);
    };
    return () => ws.close();
  }, [url]);

  return { data, status, send: wsRef.current?.send };
}
  • WebSocket connection managed in useEffect with proper cleanup
  • Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff on disconnect
  • Status tracking for UI indicators (connected/reconnecting/offline)
WebSocket useEffect cleanup Reconnection
05 / AUTH

Auth Flow (OAuth + JWT)

SaaS apps, protected routes, role-based access
const AuthContext = createContext<AuthState>(null!);

function AuthProvider({ children }) {
  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);

  useEffect(() => {
    // Check for existing session on mount
    const token = localStorage.getItem("token");
    if (token) validateToken(token).then(setUser);
    setLoading(false);
  }, []);

  const login = async (creds) => {
    const { user, token } = await api.login(creds);
    localStorage.setItem("token", token);
    setUser(user);
  };

  return (
    <AuthContext.Provider value={{ user, login, logout, loading }}>
      {children}
    </AuthContext.Provider>
  );
}
  • Context provides auth state to entire app without prop drilling
  • Loading state prevents flash of unauthenticated content
  • Token refresh logic in an axios/fetch interceptor
Context JWT Protected Routes
06 / UPLOAD

File Upload with Progress

Media uploads, document management, CMS
function useFileUpload(endpoint: string) {
  const [files, setFiles] = useState<UploadFile[]>([]);

  const upload = async (file: File) => {
    const id = crypto.randomUUID();
    setFiles(prev => [...prev, {
      id, name: file.name, progress: 0, status: "uploading"
    }]);

    const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
    xhr.upload.onprogress = (e) => {
      const pct = Math.round((e.loaded / e.total) * 100);
      setFiles(prev => prev.map(f =>
        f.id === id ? { ...f, progress: pct } : f
      ));
    };
    xhr.onload = () => updateStatus(id, "done");
    xhr.onerror = () => updateStatus(id, "error");

    const form = new FormData();
    form.append("file", file);
    xhr.open("POST", endpoint);
    xhr.send(form);
  };

  return { files, upload, removeFile };
}
  • XMLHttpRequest for upload progress (fetch doesn't support it)
  • Immutable state updates: map over files array to update progress
  • Supports multiple concurrent uploads with unique IDs
XMLHttpRequest FormData Progress Events
07 / INTERACTION

Drag and Drop

Kanban boards, sortable lists, layout builders
function useDragDrop<T>(items: T[]) {
  const [dragIdx, setDragIdx] = useState<number | null>(null);
  const [overIdx, setOverIdx] = useState<number | null>(null);

  const getHandlers = (index: number) => ({
    draggable: true,
    onDragStart: () => setDragIdx(index),
    onDragOver: (e) => { e.preventDefault(); setOverIdx(index); },
    onDrop: () => {
      if (dragIdx === null) return;
      const reordered = [...items];
      const [moved] = reordered.splice(dragIdx, 1);
      reordered.splice(index, 0, moved);
      onReorder(reordered);
      setDragIdx(null);
    },
    onDragEnd: () => { setDragIdx(null); setOverIdx(null); },
  });

  return { getHandlers, dragIdx, overIdx };
}
  • Native HTML drag and drop API -- no library needed for basics
  • getHandlers pattern returns all event props for a given index
  • Visual feedback via dragIdx/overIdx for CSS styling
HTML DnD API Reorder Logic Custom Hook
08 / EDITOR

Markdown Editor with Preview

CMS, documentation, blog platforms
function MarkdownEditor() {
  const [md, setMd] = useState("");
  const deferredMd = useDeferredValue(md);

  const html = useMemo(
    () => marked(deferredMd, { sanitize: true }),
    [deferredMd]
  );

  return (
    <div className="editor-grid">
      <textarea
        value={md}
        onChange={e => setMd(e.target.value)}
        className="editor-input"
      />
      <div
        className="preview"
        dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: html }}
      />
    </div>
  );
}
  • useDeferredValue keeps typing responsive while preview updates lag
  • useMemo prevents re-parsing markdown on unrelated renders
  • Sanitized HTML output prevents XSS from user markdown
useDeferredValue useMemo dangerouslySetInnerHTML
09 / FEEDBACK

Notification / Toast System

Success messages, errors, alerts
type Toast = {
  id: string; message: string;
  type: "success" | "error" | "info";
};

const ToastContext = createContext<{
  toast: (msg: string, type?: Toast["type"]) => void;
}>(null!);

function ToastProvider({ children }) {
  const [toasts, setToasts] = useState<Toast[]>([]);

  const toast = useCallback((msg, type = "info") => {
    const id = crypto.randomUUID();
    setToasts(prev => [...prev, { id, message: msg, type }]);
    setTimeout(() => {
      setToasts(prev => prev.filter(t => t.id !== id));
    }, 5000);
  }, []);

  return (
    <ToastContext.Provider value={{ toast }}>
      {children}
      {createPortal(<ToastStack toasts={toasts} />,
        document.body)}
    </ToastContext.Provider>
  );
}
  • createPortal renders toasts outside the component tree (at body level)
  • Auto-dismiss with setTimeout, unique IDs prevent race conditions
  • Context-based API: call toast() from anywhere in the app
createPortal Context useCallback
10 / DESIGN SYSTEM

Theme System (Dark/Light)

Design systems, multi-brand apps
function useTheme() {
  const [theme, setTheme] = useState(() => {
    if (typeof window === "undefined") return "dark";
    return localStorage.getItem("theme")
      ?? (matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)").matches
        ? "dark" : "light");
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    document.documentElement.dataset.theme = theme;
    localStorage.setItem("theme", theme);
  }, [theme]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const mq = matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: dark)");
    const handler = (e) =>
      setTheme(e.matches ? "dark" : "light");
    mq.addEventListener("change", handler);
    return () => mq.removeEventListener("change", handler);
  }, []);

  return { theme, setTheme, toggle: () =>
    setTheme(t => t === "dark" ? "light" : "dark")
  };
}
  • Lazy initializer reads localStorage and prefers-color-scheme on mount
  • Listens for OS theme changes via matchMedia change event
  • data-theme attribute on html enables CSS variable switching
matchMedia localStorage CSS Variables
11 / ACCESSIBILITY

Keyboard Shortcuts

Productivity apps, editors, power-user tools
type Shortcut = {
  key: string; ctrl?: boolean; shift?: boolean;
  handler: () => void; description: string;
};

function useKeyboardShortcuts(shortcuts: Shortcut[]) {
  useEffect(() => {
    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement) return;
      if (e.target instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement) return;

      const match = shortcuts.find(s =>
        s.key.toLowerCase() === e.key.toLowerCase() &&
        !!s.ctrl === (e.ctrlKey || e.metaKey) &&
        !!s.shift === e.shiftKey
      );
      if (match) { e.preventDefault(); match.handler(); }
    };

    window.addEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
    return () => window.removeEventListener("keydown", handleKeyDown);
  }, [shortcuts]);
}
  • Ignores shortcuts when user is typing in inputs/textareas
  • Supports modifier keys (Ctrl/Cmd, Shift) with platform normalization
  • Description field enables a keyboard shortcut help dialog
KeyboardEvent useEffect a11y
12 / UX

Optimistic Updates

Likes, bookmarks, toggles, real-time collaboration
function useLike(postId: string) {
  const [liked, setLiked] = useState(false);
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const toggleLike = async () => {
    // Optimistic: update UI immediately
    const prevLiked = liked;
    const prevCount = count;
    setLiked(!liked);
    setCount(c => liked ? c - 1 : c + 1);

    try {
      await api.toggleLike(postId);
    } catch {
      // Rollback on failure
      setLiked(prevLiked);
      setCount(prevCount);
      toast.error("Failed to update");
    }
  };

  return { liked, count, toggleLike };
}

// React 19: useOptimistic hook built-in
  • Instant UI feedback: state updates before API response
  • Rollback pattern: capture previous state, restore on error
  • React 19's useOptimistic hook automates this pattern
Optimistic UI Error Rollback useOptimistic
13 / SEARCH

Search with Debounce

Search bars, autocomplete, filtering
function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number): T {
  const [debounced, setDebounced] = useState(value);
  useEffect(() => {
    const timer = setTimeout(() => setDebounced(value), delay);
    return () => clearTimeout(timer);
  }, [value, delay]);
  return debounced;
}

function SearchBar() {
  const [query, setQuery] = useState("");
  const debouncedQuery = useDebounce(query, 300);
  const { data } = useSWR(
    debouncedQuery ? `/api/search?q=${debouncedQuery}` : null
  );

  return (
    <>
      <input value={query}
        onChange={e => setQuery(e.target.value)} />
      <Results data={data} />
    </>
  );
}
  • useDebounce hook delays value updates by N ms after last change
  • Effect cleanup clears pending timeout on each keystroke
  • SWR/React Query with null key skips fetch when query is empty
Debounce SWR Effect Cleanup
14 / UI

Modal / Dialog System

Confirmations, forms, detail views
function Modal({ open, onClose, children }) {
  const dialogRef = useRef<HTMLDialogElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const dialog = dialogRef.current;
    if (!dialog) return;
    if (open) dialog.showModal();
    else dialog.close();
  }, [open]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const dialog = dialogRef.current;
    if (!dialog) return;
    const handleClose = () => onClose();
    dialog.addEventListener("close", handleClose);
    return () => dialog.removeEventListener("close", handleClose);
  }, [onClose]);

  return (
    <dialog ref={dialogRef} className="modal">
      <button onClick={onClose} className="close">x</button>
      {children}
    </dialog>
  );
}
  • Native <dialog> element: built-in focus trap, Escape key, backdrop
  • showModal() prevents interaction with content behind the dialog
  • Event listener on "close" handles both Escape key and button clicks
dialog element useRef Focus Trap
15 / PERFORMANCE

Virtualized List

Feeds, logs, large datasets, file explorers
function VirtualList<T>({
  items, rowHeight, overscan = 5,
  renderRow
}: VirtualListProps<T>) {
  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
  const [scrollTop, setScrollTop] = useState(0);
  const containerHeight = useContainerHeight(containerRef);

  const totalHeight = items.length * rowHeight;
  const startIdx = Math.max(0,
    Math.floor(scrollTop / rowHeight) - overscan);
  const endIdx = Math.min(items.length,
    Math.ceil((scrollTop + containerHeight) / rowHeight) + overscan);
  const visibleItems = items.slice(startIdx, endIdx);

  return (
    <div ref={containerRef} onScroll={handleScroll}
      style={{ height: "100%", overflow: "auto" }}>
      <div style={{ height: totalHeight }}>
        {visibleItems.map((item, i) =>
          renderRow(item, startIdx + i)
        )}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
  • Only renders visible items + overscan buffer (20-30 DOM nodes for 10K items)
  • Outer div with totalHeight maintains correct scrollbar size
  • Absolute positioning offsets visible rows to their correct scroll position
Windowing Overscan Scroll Position
